title: Houthis fire first ballistic missile at Israel — Day 29 opens 5th active front in war
description: Yemen's Houthi movement fired a barrage of ballistic missiles at Israel on the morning of Day 29 — the first Houthi attack on Israel since the Iran-Israel war began on February 28. Houthi Brig. Gen. Yahya Saree announced the operation on Al Masirah TV, citing Israel's continued attacks on Iran, Lebanon, and Iraq. Missiles targeted sensitive military sites in southern Israel with Beersheba as the primary target area. Air raid sirens blared around Beersheba, near the Negev Nuclear Research Center (Dimona area). The IDF confirmed it intercepted the incoming missiles. No casualties or significant damage reported. Senior Houthi official Mohammed Mansour stated closing the Bab al-Mandeb Strait is among their options — threatening a second major shipping chokepoint alongside Hormuz.

title: Iran drone strike hits Salalah Port, Oman — crane destroyed, operations suspended
description: Two Iranian drones struck Oman's Port of Salalah in the pre-dawn hours of Day 29, one of the largest container ports in the Arabian Sea. One drone hit a large container crane, breaking the crane arms and causing structural damage. One foreign expatriate worker suffered moderate injuries. Maersk confirmed all crew safe and no Maersk vessels directly affected but the shipping giant suspended port operations for approximately 48 hours for damage assessment. The IRGC separately claimed to have targeted a US military logistics support vessel near Salalah — US has not confirmed. Oman condemned the strikes as a violation of its sovereignty. The attack was part of a coordinated Gulf infrastructure blitz by Iran that also hit Kuwait International Airport and a UAE industrial zone in the same dawn window.

title: Vance: US 'taking care of business' in Iran — will be out 'soon'

Vance said the US had accomplished the vast majority of its military objectives and that the war would continue only a little while longer to ensure Iran is 'neutered for a very, very long time.' He also acknowledged Iran is still attempting to build a nuclear weapon. Separately, Secretary of State Rubio said at a G7 press conference in France that the war would conclude 'in a matter of weeks, not months,' with the aim of destroying Iran's ability to manufacture missiles, drones, and missile launchers. Iran has reportedly told US officials it prefers Vance — not Witkoff or Kushner — to lead any US negotiating delegation.

A separate missile fragment hit the ADAMA (Syngenta subsidiary) Makhteshim agricultural chemical plant in the nearby Ne'ot Hovav industrial zone, starting a fire and triggering fears of a hazardous chemical leak. Home Front Command ordered residents to stay indoors and close windows; three hazardous materials teams deployed; Highway 40 closed. Environmental Protection Minister Idit Silman later issued an all-clear stating no risk to the public.

This is the fourth such incident since the war began, raising concerns about Iran's missile accuracy and the risk of spillover into NATO territory.

Al-Monitor — Turkey says NATO defenses intercepted fourth missile

title: Israel's Knesset approves record $45.8B defense budget in 62-55 vote — prevents snap elections
description: Israel's Knesset approved the 2026 state budget in a 62-55 vote late Sunday, featuring a record $45.8 billion defense allocation including NIS 30B+ for 'Operation Roaring Lion.' The total budget is approximately $271 billion. The approval prevents snap elections that polls indicated Netanyahu would lose. Opposition leader Lapid called it 'the greatest theft in the state's history.' Ultra-Orthodox parties secured $750M+ in additional funding as a coalition condition.

title: IMF warns 'all roads lead to higher prices and slower growth' — largest oil disruption in history
description: The IMF issued a major warning on March 30 that the Iran war is causing the 'largest disruption to the global oil market in history.' IMF economists wrote that 'all roads lead to higher prices and slower growth.' Every 10% oil price increase pushes up global inflation by 0.4% and reduces GDP by 0.2%. The OECD raised US inflation forecast to 4.2% for 2026. Goldman Sachs puts US recession probability at 30% over next 12 months.

sources.name: Bloomberg — IMF Says Iran War Means Higher Prices, Slower Growth Worldwide
Al-Monitor — Iran war is dimming outlook for many economies, IMF says

title: IDF completes 'wide-scale' 170-target strike wave across Tehran — hits missile sites, IRGC university, plutonium facility
description: The IDF announced completion of a wide-scale wave of 170 strikes targeting Iranian military infrastructure across Tehran and other cities. Targets included a ballistic missile component development site, UAV engine production facility, IRGC military industries complex, SA-Iran (Iran Electronics Industries) building, Imam Hussein University (main IRGC academic institution), and a facility designed to produce weapons-grade plutonium. Strikes also hit targets in Tabriz, Karaj, Yazd, Qom, Bandar Abbas, Parchin, and Kish Island. Power blackout reported in Tehran from strikes on electrical facilities.

title: Israeli strike hits orphanage west of Tehran — 2 killed, 5 injured

Fire was later contained. 6 impact sites reported: 3 in Kiryat Ata, 2 in Haifa, 1 in Shfaram. 2 people injured (1 seriously) in the broader barrage. Second strike on the facility during this war (first was March 19). Energy Minister said no damage to production facilities.

Maxsim Entis (21, Bat Yam), and a 4th soldier. 1 soldier seriously wounded, 1 reservist moderately wounded. IDF responded with tank fire and airstrikes.

Iranian state media: 'The American-Zionist enemy has carried out several attacks on Kharg Island.' No immediate reports of casualties or confirmed damage to oil infrastructure. Kharg handles ~90% of Iran's oil exports.

sources.name: LBC — Kharg Island strikes
AzerNews — Kharg Island attacks

title: Iranian missile barrage targets southern Israel — air defenses intercept
description: Iran launched new round of missiles toward southern Israel on April 7. Sirens sounded across southern Israel including Beersheba. Israeli military reported air defenses successfully intercepted the threats. No casualties reported from this barrage.

ABC News — Iran live updates

title: IRGC Intelligence Chief Khademi and Quds Force commander Bagheri killed in Israeli strike
description: Maj. Gen. Majid Khademi, IRGC intelligence chief and effectively No. 2 in the IRGC, killed in Israeli precision strike. Quds Force commander Asghar Bagheri also killed in the same operation. Continues pattern of Israeli targeted assassinations of senior Iranian military leadership.

title: Armed attackers open fire near Israeli consulate in Istanbul — 1 killed, 2 captured
description: Three assailants with long-barreled weapons opened fire at police outside the Israeli consulate building in Istanbul. 6-minute gunfight ensued. One attacker killed, two captured with injuries. Two Turkish police officers slightly injured. The consulate was empty (Israel recalled staff in 2023). Attackers arrived from Izmit in a rented car; one had ties to a religious extremist organization.

title: King Fahd Causeway temporarily closed after Iranian missile threat to Bahrain

The closure came after Iran launched 7 ballistic missiles at Saudi Arabia earlier. Causeway later reopened.

IRGC warned it will 'act against American infrastructure and its partners in a way that will deprive them of regional oil and gas for years.'

title: Trump agrees to 2-week suspension of bombing Iran
description: After the 8PM ET deadline passed, Trump posted on Truth Social agreeing to 'suspend the bombing and attack of Iran for a period of two weeks,' conditional on Iran agreeing to the 'COMPLETE, IMMEDIATE, and SAFE OPENING of the Strait of Hormuz.' Pakistan PM Sharif mediated, asking both sides for a two-week ceasefire window. Bloomberg described it as a 'two-week ceasefire with Iran to finalize talks.' Major de-escalation signal after 39 days of war.

title: Iran's Supreme National Security Council formally accepts two-week ceasefire

FM Araghchi confirmed: 'For a period of two weeks, safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz will be possible via coordination with Iran's Armed Forces and with due consideration of technical limitations.' Iran also stated: 'If attacks against Iran are halted, our Powerful Armed Forces will cease their defensive operations.' China reportedly gave Khamenei regime a 'last-minute nudge' to accept.

Al Jazeera — Trump suspends bombing, Iran agrees to reopen Hormuz

title: Oil prices crash 16-19% on ceasefire news — biggest one-day drop since 1991 Gulf War
description: In after-hours trading following the ceasefire announcement, Brent crude plunged ~16% to ~$93/bbl and WTI dropped ~19% to ~$92/bbl — described as the biggest one-day oil price free fall since the 1991 Gulf War. S&P 500 futures surged 2.4%, Dow futures rose 1,000+ points, Nasdaq futures gained 3%. However, by April 8 morning trading, prices rebounded to Brent ~$108-110 and WTI ~$112-115 as markets expressed skepticism about ceasefire durability.

NBC News — Oil prices plunge 15%, stock futures rally

Sharif stated: 'Both parties have displayed remarkable wisdom.' Field Marshal Asim Munir was reportedly in contact 'all night' with Vance and Witkoff to finalize arrangements. The talks aim to reach a 'conclusive agreement to settle all disputes' within the two-week ceasefire window.

Jubail petrochemical area in Saudi Arabia struck. Fire at UAE's Habshan gas processing facility from debris. 4 lightly injured in Tel Sheva, Israel.

title: Trump warns US military 'remains in place' around Iran — threatens resumption
description: Trump posted on Truth Social that 'All U.S. Ships, Aircraft, and Military Personnel, with additional Ammunition, Weaponry, and anything else appropriate and necessary for the lethal prosecution and destruction of an already substantially degraded Enemy, will remain in place in, and around, Iran, until such time as the REAL AGREEMENT is fully complied with.' Warned if not complied with, 'the Shootin Starts, bigger, and better, and stronger than anyone has ever seen before.'

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Iran war live April 9
NBC News — Iran war ceasefire live updates

title: IRGC publishes naval mine map — reveals 'danger zone' in Strait of Hormuz
description: Iran's IRGC Navy posted a map via semi-official ISNA and Tasnim news agencies showing a large circle marked 'danger zone' in Farsi over the Traffic Separation Scheme — the standard route ships take through the Strait of Hormuz. The map directs vessels to alternative routes north and south of Larak Island, requiring coordination with IRGC forces. Mines identified by OSINT as Maham-3 and Maham-7 sensor-equipped weapons. Statement warns: 'Due to the past war situation and possible anti-ship mines, all vessels are advised to coordinate with the IRGC Navy and use alternative routes until further notice.'

sources.name: Times of Israel — IRGC releases map to guide ships around Hormuz mines
CBS News — Iran ceasefire live updates

title: Hezbollah rocket barrages escalate on northern Israel — 4 injured, 7+ waves
description: Hezbollah fired multiple rocket barrages at northern Israel throughout Thursday morning, with sirens sounding at least seven times. Targets included Manara, Nahariya, and Kibbutz Kabri in the Western Galilee. Three people injured in Nahariya and one in Kibbutz Kabri. IDF reported dozens of launches from Lebanon since midnight. Hezbollah claimed attacks in response to 'Israeli ceasefire violations' after Operation Eternal Darkness, stating: 'This response will continue until the Israeli-American aggression against our country and our people ceases.'

sources.name: Rappler — Hezbollah rockets northern Israel
Times of Israel — Sirens sound in northern towns

title: Israeli airstrike on Abbassiyeh kills 7 — continued Lebanon operations
description: Israeli warplanes struck the southern Lebanese town of Abbassiyeh on Thursday, killing at least seven people and wounding several others, according to Lebanon's National News Agency. The strike was part of a broader wave of attacks on southern Lebanese towns including Kafra, Jmaijmeh, Safad al-Battikh, Majdal Selm, and Deir Antar near Qasmiyeh bridge. Artillery shelling also damaged the town of Haris. Israel continued to assert that the ceasefire does not cover Lebanon.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Iran war live April 9
Reuters — Lebanon strikes continue

title: Houthi anti-ship missile targets Maersk Yorktown in Gulf of Aden — intercepted
description: The US-flagged container ship Maersk Yorktown was targeted by a Houthi anti-ship missile while transiting the Gulf of Aden under escort by destroyers USS Mason and USS Laboon. The missile was intercepted before impact. Houthis also claimed attacks on vessels MSC Gina and MSC Darwin (claimed Israeli-owned) and a US destroyer. This follows Houthi attacks on three commercial vessels and two US frigates on April 7.

sources.name: CNN — Day 40 Middle East conflict live
Al Jazeera — Iran war live April 9

title: House Democrats attempt War Powers Resolution during pro forma session
description: House Democrats attempted to pass a War Powers Resolution to limit President Trump's authority to strike Iran, via unanimous consent during a pro forma session at 11:30 AM on April 9. House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries demanded passage of the resolution introduced by Ranking Member Greg Meeks. The House is in two-week recess with votes not scheduled until April 14. Republican leadership was expected to block the effort. Rep. Glenn Ivey (D-MD) led the floor effort.

sources.name: NBC News — House Democrats war powers Iran
CBS News — Iran ceasefire live updates

title: IDF Division 162 deploys to southern Lebanon — buffer zone expansion
description: The Israeli military announced that troops from Division 162 would operate in southern Lebanon 'with the aim of expanding' the buffer zone. This joins five divisions already operating (91st, 36th, 146th, 162nd, 98th). PM Netanyahu said the military planned to create 'a larger buffer zone' to push back Hezbollah missile threat. IDF advancing north past coastal Naqoura toward Bayada, with media reporting no plans to push deeper than ~20km north of the border.

sources.name: CNN — Day 40 Middle East conflict live
Al Jazeera — Iran war live April 9

title: Tehran — massive crowds mark 40th day since Ayatollah Khamenei assassination
description: Large crowds of Iranians gathered in the streets of Tehran on April 9 to mark the 40th day since Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in the initial US-Israeli strikes on February 28. The gathering came on the first full day of the ceasefire, with some traffic returning to Tehran's streets during daylight. Iran's Supreme National Security Council called on citizens to remain united until 'victory is finalized.'

sources.name: PBS News — Iran ceasefire
CBS News — Iran ceasefire live updates

title: Iran re-closes Strait of Hormuz — cites Israeli Lebanon strikes as ceasefire violation
description: Iran's IRGC claimed shipping through the Strait of Hormuz stopped following Israel's bombardment of Lebanon on April 8, which Tehran said violated the ceasefire. Press TV reported the strait was 'fully closed' with oil tanker AUROURA making a 180-degree turn near the Musandam coast. Fars news agency confirmed tanker movements halted 'concurrent with Israel's fresh deadly attacks on Lebanon.' White House Press Secretary Leavitt said any closure was 'completely unacceptable.' Only 2-3 ships had transited since the ceasefire, with 800+ freighters still trapped inside the Gulf.

sources.name: CNN — Hormuz traffic negligible after ceasefire
CBS News — Iran ceasefire live updates

title: IDF kills Hezbollah chief Qassem's secretary/nephew Ali Yusuf Harshi in Beirut airstrike
description: IDF confirmed it killed Ali Yusuf Harshi, the personal secretary and nephew of Hezbollah Secretary-General Naim Qassem, in a strike on Beirut's Tallet Khayat neighborhood on the evening of April 8. Initial Reuters flash erroneously reported Qassem himself killed — later retracted. IDF says Harshi was a key communications conduit for Qassem. Also killed Lebanese Resistance Brigade commander Maher Qassem Hamdan in a separate Sidon strike.

sources.name: Times of Israel — IDF says it killed Hezbollah chief's secretary
Jerusalem Post — Naim Qassem's secretary and nephew killed

title: Hezbollah fires ~70 rockets at northern Israel throughout Day 41
description: Hezbollah fired approximately 70 rockets from southern Lebanon at northern Israel throughout April 9, targeting Kiryat Shmona, Galilee, Taibe, and Manara. One rocket hit a home in Kiryat Shmona (no injuries from that hit). Hezbollah claimed attacks were in response to Israeli 'ceasefire violations' in Lebanon. IDF reported sirens 7+ times. Four people injured earlier in the day (3 Nahariya, 1 Kabri) — no additional casualties from evening volleys.

sources.name: Times of Israel — 70 rockets fired from Lebanon
Haaretz — Hezbollah says fired rockets in response to ceasefire violations

title: Netanyahu orders direct negotiations with Lebanon on Hezbollah disarmament
description: Netanyahu instructed cabinet to begin direct negotiations with Lebanon 'as soon as possible,' focusing on Hezbollah disarmament and peace. Decision came after Trump phone call pressing Netanyahu to scale back Lebanon strikes. First meeting planned next week at US State Department — Israel's Ambassador Yechiel Leiter and Lebanon's Ambassador Nada Hamadeh-Moawad to lead delegations. Netanyahu said negotiations would happen 'under fire' — no Lebanon ceasefire.

sources.name: Axios — Netanyahu announces negotiations with Lebanon
Al Jazeera — Netanyahu ready for talks with Lebanon ASAP

title: Chinese VLCCs test Hormuz transit — 3 ships attempt exit with Iraqi/Saudi crude
description: Two fully laden Chinese VLCCs (Cospearl Lake and He Rong Hai) positioned near Hormuz to test exit; a third (Yuan Hua Hu) approached hours later. Two carry Iraqi crude, one Saudi. China's Foreign Ministry confirmed three ships recently passed through. Iraq told traders Iranian exemption allows Iraqi oil transits. Iran still demands all ships coordinate with Tehran for passage.

sources.name: Bloomberg — China tankers join queue to test Hormuz exit
SCMP — 3 Chinese ships exit Strait of Hormuz

title: Mojtaba Khamenei vows revenge, pledges 'new phase' for Strait of Hormuz management
description: Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, in a statement read on state TV, vowed to 'take revenge' for his father and all killed in the war. Said Iran would bring management of the Strait of Hormuz 'into a new phase.' Warned Gulf neighbors Iran is 'still awaiting an appropriate reaction' from them. Statement came as ceasefire frays over Lebanon question.

sources.name: CNN — Live updates April 9
Republic World — Mojtaba Khamenei new phase Hormuz

title: EU's Kallas arrives in Riyadh for diplomatic push to transform ceasefire into lasting settlement
description: EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas arrived in Riyadh for diplomatic push to transform the temporary US-Iran truce into a lasting settlement. Held talks with Saudi FM Prince Faisal bin Farhan and GCC Secretary-General. Called ceasefire 'a step back from the brink' and said Iran truce 'should extend to Lebanon.' Kallas urged Hezbollah disarmament.

sources.name: ANI News — Kallas urges permanent peace during Riyadh mission

title: Trump meets NATO chief Rutte, slams alliance, revives Greenland threat
description: Trump held 2-hour White House meeting with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte. Slammed NATO for not joining Iran war: 'NATO wasn't there when we needed them.' Rutte acknowledged: 'He is clearly disappointed with many NATO allies, and I can see his point.' Trump revived Greenland acquisition threat and reportedly discussed possible US withdrawal from NATO. Bloomberg reports Trump floated leaving the alliance.

sources.name: NPR — Trump meets with NATO chief
Bloomberg — Trump discusses leaving NATO

title: IRGC shoots down Israeli Hermes 900 drone over Fars Province
description: Iran's IRGC announced it intercepted and destroyed an Israeli Hermes 900 drone in the skies over Lar, Fars Province, using its integrated air defense system. IRGC warned that 'the entry of any enemy aircraft into Iranian airspace — even without military operations — constitutes a violation of the ceasefire.'

sources.name: Times of Israel — IRGC shoots down Israeli drone

title: Houthi missile targets Maersk Yorktown in Gulf of Aden — intercepted by USS Mason
description: Houthis fired an anti-ship missile at the US-flagged container ship Maersk Yorktown while being escorted by destroyers USS Mason and USS Laboon in the Gulf of Aden. The missile was intercepted before it could cause damage. Houthis later claimed responsibility and also targeted MSC Gina and MSC Darwin.

sources.name: Wikipedia — 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis

title: IDF 98th Division captures nearly all of Bint Jbeil in southern Lebanon
description: Jerusalem Post reported that the IDF's 98th Division had almost fully taken control of the town of Bint Jbeil in southern Lebanon. IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir visited troops on the outskirts, stating 'Iran is much weaker' and Israel is 'prepared to return to combat at any moment.' Hezbollah operatives engaged IDF soldiers with RPGs and light arms.

title: Hezbollah fires missile at Ashdod — deepest strike attempt intercepted by Iron Dome
description: Hezbollah launched a missile at the southern coastal city of Ashdod, marking one of the deepest attacks attempted by Hezbollah during the current fighting. The missile was intercepted by the IDF. Sirens activated in Tel Aviv and surrounding towns due to falling fragments. No injuries reported.

sources.name: Times of Israel — Hezbollah missile at Ashdod intercepted

title: Israeli strike on al-Abbassieh kills at least 7, wounds several
description: Lebanon's National News Agency reported that an Israeli airstrike on the town of al-Abbassieh in southern Lebanon killed at least 7 people and wounded several others. Part of broader wave of continued Israeli strikes across Lebanon on Day 42 of the conflict.

title: Qasmiyeh Bridge near Tyre damaged — southern Lebanon severed from humanitarian aid
description: Israeli strikes damaged the Qasmiyeh Bridge, the last main crossing point into the area south of the Litani River. Lebanese Armed Forces evacuated the bridge after an Israeli airstrike destroyed a footbridge that fell onto the main bridge. Human Rights Watch warns tens of thousands cut off from food and healthcare. Local authorities say food supplies would last one week.

sources.name: PBS — Israeli strikes kill more than 300

title: IMF chief Georgieva warns: 'All roads lead to higher prices and slower growth'
description: IMF Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva warned that the Iran war could trigger a global inflation crisis. IMF will downgrade global growth forecast next week from 3.3%. Every 10% oil price rise adds 0.4% to global inflation and subtracts 0.2% from GDP. Urged central banks to hike rates if inflation expectations 'break anchor.' Goldman Sachs puts US recession probability at 30%.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — IMF warns of looming inflation crisis
The National — IMF chief warns of higher interest rates

title: Day 42: Vance delegation en route to Islamabad for historic US-Iran peace talks
description: VP JD Vance, envoy Steve Witkoff, and Jared Kushner head to Islamabad, Pakistan for Saturday talks — first in-person US-Iran negotiations since war began. Iran's delegation led by Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf and FM Araghchi. Pakistani PM Sharif mediating. Key sticking points: nuclear enrichment, Lebanon inclusion, Hormuz control, sanctions relief. Trump 'very optimistic' about deal.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — US-Iran talks in Pakistan
Axios — US-Iran peace talks Vance Pakistan
NBC News — Trump optimistic about Iran peace deal

title: Hegseth declares 'historic victory' — says Iran 'begged for this ceasefire'
description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth declared 'Operation Epic Fury was a historic and overwhelming victory on the battlefield.' Said Iran's military rendered 'combat ineffective for years to come.' Urged Iran to hand over 970 lbs of highly enriched uranium, warning Trump could authorize Special Operations to recover it. Said US forces 'not going anywhere.'

title: Former Iran FM Kamal Kharazi dies from April 1 airstrike wounds
description: Kamal Kharazi, 81, former Iranian Foreign Minister (1997-2005) and adviser to Supreme Leader Khamenei, died from wounds sustained in a US-Israeli airstrike on his Tehran home on April 1. His wife was killed in the same strike. Kharazi was one of the most senior Iranian officials to die in the conflict.

description: Saudi FM Prince Faisal bin Farhan and Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi held their first publicly announced phone call since February 28, discussing 'ways to reduce tensions to restore security and stability in the region.' Significant diplomatic breakthrough as Saudi Arabia navigates between Gulf Arab allies and Iran.

Al Arabiya — Saudi FM receives call from Iranian counterpart

title: Mojtaba Khamenei declares 'decisive victory,' announces new phase in Hormuz management
description: Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei declared a 'decisive victory' in the conflict with the US and Israel, announcing a 'new phase' in Iran's management of the Strait of Hormuz. The statement was broadcast on state TV and picked up by Chinese state media. Khamenei framed the ceasefire as validation of Iran's strategy, asserting Iran 'will not forfeit its rights.'

title: Lebanon declares national mourning day as Israeli strike toll reaches 303 in single day
description: Lebanon declared a national day of mourning after Israeli strikes killed 303 people and wounded 1,150 on April 8 — the deadliest single day of the 2026 Lebanon front. The strikes came hours after the US-Iran ceasefire was announced, with Israel asserting the truce does not cover Lebanon. Human Rights Watch condemned the attacks.

PBS — Israeli strikes kill more than 300 in one day

title: HRW: Israeli strikes destroy vital Qasmiyeh Bridge near Tyre, severing aid routes to south Lebanon
description: Human Rights Watch reported that Israeli airstrikes destroyed the Qasmiyeh Bridge near Tyre, a vital infrastructure link, effectively severing southern Lebanon from humanitarian aid delivery. HRW called for investigation of strikes that killed hundreds and damaged civilian infrastructure. The bridge destruction compounds the humanitarian crisis in southern Lebanon.

sources.name: HRW — Lebanon: Israeli strikes kill hundreds, damage vital bridge

title: Israeli strikes hit residential areas in Hanawya and Aita al-Shaab, southern Lebanon
description: Israeli forces struck residential areas in Hanawya and Aita al-Shaab in southern Lebanon on April 10, destroying homes and killing civilians. Al Jazeera reported the strikes targeted areas with no apparent military objectives. The IDF's 98th Division continued operations near Bint Jbeil with near-full control of the town.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Israel strikes residential areas in southern Lebanon

title: Russia and China veto UN Security Council resolution on Strait of Hormuz
description: Russia and China vetoed a UN Security Council resolution calling for freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz and condemning Iran's blockade. The UK expressed deep regret. The veto underscores the geopolitical divide, with Russia and China protecting Iran's leverage over the strait during ceasefire negotiations.

sources.name: UN News — Russia and China veto resolution on Strait of Hormuz

title: Trump criticizes Iran: 'doing a very poor job' of letting oil through Hormuz
description: President Trump publicly criticized Iran for 'doing a very poor job' of allowing oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz despite the ceasefire agreement. The comments came as zero oil tankers had successfully transited the strait since the ceasefire. Trump expressed frustration but said he remains 'very optimistic' about the upcoming Islamabad peace talks.

description: Israeli airstrike struck the Nabatieh Serail government building in southern Lebanon, killing 13 Lebanese State Security personnel. Lebanon's President Joseph Aoun condemned the attack on state institutions. The strike targeted the government building, not a Hezbollah facility, raising international condemnation.

Talks shifted from expected proximity format to direct negotiations. Iran's red lines include full sovereignty over Hormuz, Lebanon ceasefire, release of frozen assets, and enrichment rights. title: IDF strikes kill 10+ in southern Lebanon; Hezbollah targets Ashdod naval base with long-range missiles
description: IDF continued strikes across southern Lebanon on April 11, killing at least 10 including 3 emergency workers. Cumulative Lebanon death toll now 2,020 killed and 6,436 wounded since March 2. Separately, Hezbollah fired missiles at Israel's Ashdod naval base, triggering sirens in Tel Aviv. The missile was intercepted. Hezbollah also fired ~30 rockets at northern Israel hitting Kibbutz Misgav Am and a school in Deir al-Asad. IDF's 98th Division reported near-full control of Bint Jbeil.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Israel rejects ceasefire with Hezbollah ahead of Lebanon talks
Times of Israel — Hezbollah fires missile at Ashdod

title: CNN: US intelligence says China preparing MANPAD weapons shipment to Iran
description: CNN reported exclusively that US intelligence indicates China is preparing to deliver shoulder-fired anti-air missile systems (MANPADs) to Iran within coming weeks, routing shipments through third countries. This would mark an escalation from previously limited dual-use technology assistance. Chinese embassy denied: 'China has never provided weapons to any party to the conflict.'

sources.name: CNN — US intelligence: China preparing weapons shipment to Iran
Jerusalem Post — Report: China set to supply Iran with arms

title: Al-Aqsa Mosque reopens — 100,000+ worshippers at first Friday prayers after 40-day closure
description: Over 100,000 Muslim worshippers performed Friday prayers at Al-Aqsa Mosque in Jerusalem — the first prayers at the holy site after a 40-day closure since the war began. The mosque had been shuttered even during Ramadan, making this reopening a significant religious and political milestone.

sources.name: Times of Israel — Over 100,000 Muslim worshipers at Al-Aqsa
Free Malaysia Today — Over 100,000 worshippers at Al-Aqsa

title: Netanyahu rejects Lebanon ceasefire; corruption trial resumes Sunday
description: Israel formally refused to discuss a ceasefire with Hezbollah at upcoming Washington talks with Lebanon scheduled for Tuesday. Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors held late-night discussions Friday to finalize arrangements. Separately, Netanyahu's corruption trial resumes Sunday April 13 at 9:30 AM in Jerusalem District Court after a war-related freeze.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Israel rejects ceasefire with Hezbollah ahead of Lebanon talks
Jerusalem Post — Netanyahu trial to resume Sunday

title: Pope Leo XIV leads prayer vigil for peace at St. Peter's Basilica
description: Pope Leo XIV led a prayer vigil for peace at St. Peter's Basilica at 6:00 PM Rome time, with meditations from Church Fathers and participants from different continents lighting candles with fire from the Lamp of Peace from Assisi. Parishes and dioceses globally held concurrent vigils.

sources.name: Vatican News — Pope's prayer vigil for peace
EWTN News — Pope Leo XIV announces April 11 peace vigil

title: Tel Aviv anti-war protests — 1,000+ demonstrators at Habima Square for 6th consecutive week
description: Over 1,000 protesters gathered at Habima Square in Tel Aviv for the sixth consecutive week of anti-war, anti-government demonstrations demanding an end to military operations and a return to negotiations.

title: Trump issues 24-hour deadline warning — warships 'reloaded with best ammunition' if talks fail
description: President Trump warned the US will 'find out in about 24 hours' whether the Islamabad talks succeed, saying warships are being reloaded with 'the best ammunition' and strikes could resume if negotiations fail. Trump also announced the Hormuz mine clearing, posting: 'We're now starting the process of clearing out the Strait of Hormuz as a favor to Countries all over the World.'

title: Islamabad talks Day 1 concludes after 14 hours with no deal — Hormuz control key sticking point
description: First direct US-Iran face-to-face negotiations since 1979 concluded Day 1 after 14+ hours across three rounds with no agreement. VP Vance led US side; Iran's 71-member delegation led by Ghalibaf and Araghchi. Some progress on Lebanon ceasefire conditions and frozen Iranian assets, but stalemate persists over Strait of Hormuz sovereignty. Iran posted: 'Negotiations will continue despite some remaining differences.' Day 2 set for Sunday.

Washington Post — Direct U.S.-Iran talks stretch well past midnight

title: Trump: 'Whether we make a deal or not makes no difference to me'
description: President Trump told reporters he does not 'care' what happens with Iran negotiations because 'regardless what happens, we win.' He stated warships are being reloaded and the Strait will reopen 'fairly soon — with or without Iran's cooperation.' Also posted: 'Our warships are loading up with the best weapons ever made.'

title: Pope Leo XIV denounces 'delusion of omnipotence' fueling US-Israeli war at peace vigil
description: Pope Leo XIV presided over an evening Prayer Vigil for Peace at St. Peter's Basilica, his strongest statement yet on the war. He denounced the 'delusion of omnipotence' fueling the conflict and called praying for peace a way to 'break the demonic cycle of evil.' Did not name Trump or the US directly but widely interpreted as directed at Washington.

title: Rubio arrests 3 Iranian nationals tied to 1979 hostage crisis figure 'Screaming Mary'
description: Secretary of State Rubio announced the arrest of three Iranian nationals — Seyed Eissa Hashemi, Maryam Tahmasebi, and their son — whose green cards he had revoked. Eissa Hashemi is the son of Masoumeh Ebtekar ('Screaming Mary'), the spokeswoman for the 1979 Embassy hostage-takers. They entered the US in 2014 on Obama-era visas. ICE detained all three.

title: EU sanctions 19 Iranian officials and entities for human rights violations
description: EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas announced new EU sanctions targeting 19 regime officials and entities responsible for serious human rights violations. Kallas also reiterated the call for the ceasefire to extend to Lebanon and urged adherence to freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz.

description: Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei warned that 'Our hands are on the trigger' and said Iran would move into a 'new phase' without elaboration. He claimed Iran achieved a 'final victory' and 'astonished the world' during the conflict, marking 40 days since his father Ali Khamenei was killed. Stated: 'We will certainly not leave the criminal aggressors who attacked our country unpunished.'

title: Iran lost track of mine locations in Strait of Hormuz — mines drifted from original positions
description: US officials revealed that Iran cannot locate many of the 2,000-6,000 sea mines it deployed in the Strait of Hormuz — they have drifted from original positions. Iran released navigational maps acknowledging the mines for the first time. Mine clearance now estimated to take 6-10 weeks. Only 9 commercial ships transited on Thursday.

Euronews — Is Iran unable to locate its mines in the Strait of Hormuz?

title: Netanyahu demands Hezbollah disarmament for Lebanon peace — wants deal 'for generations'
description: Netanyahu issued a televised statement saying he wants a Lebanon peace deal 'that will last for generations' but set two preconditions: dismantling of Hezbollah's weapons and a 'real peace agreement.' Stated: 'I have given my approval [for talks with Lebanon], but on two conditions.' Lebanon-Israel-US talks confirmed for Tuesday in Washington.

Al Jazeera — Netanyahu calls for talks with Lebanon 'as soon as possible'

title: Pezeshkian vows Iran will 'negotiate bravely' — backs Islamabad delegation

title: Guterres welcomes Islamabad talks, warns Lebanon fighting endangers truce
description: UN Secretary-General Guterres, through his spokesperson, welcomed the Islamabad talks and called on parties to 'seize this diplomatic opportunity to engage in good faith toward a lasting and comprehensive agreement.' He also warned that 'ongoing military activity in Lebanon poses a grave risk to the ceasefire' between the US and Iran.

title: Islamabad talks collapse after 21 hours — Vance departs with no deal, leaves 'final and best offer'
description: VP JD Vance held a press conference at Islamabad's Serena Hotel after 21 hours of marathon negotiations ended without a deal. Vance stated: 'We have not reached an agreement. I think that's bad news for Iran much more than for the United States.' He described a 'final and best offer' left on the table. Key sticking points: Iran's refusal to commit long-term to not pursuing nuclear weapons, control of Strait of Hormuz, and status of Lebanon operations. Vance departed aboard Air Force Two at 07:08 local time. Iran's Foreign Ministry responded that 'the ball is in America's court' and said the two sides 'agreed on several points' but 'disagreements on two important issues' remain.

title: IRGC issues 'last warning' to US warships — denies Hormuz transit, vows 'strong response'
description: The IRGC issued a statement 'strongly denying' US claims of warship transit through the Strait of Hormuz and warning of a 'strong response' to any foreign military vessels. IRGC spokesperson stated: 'The initiative for the passage and movement of any vessel is in the hands of the Armed Forces of the Islamic Republic of Iran.' This follows the transit of USS Frank E. Peterson (DDG-121) and USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112) as part of CENTCOM mine clearance operations. Murphy turned ON its AIS beacon — breaking typical Navy protocol — as a deliberate signal.

ANI — Iran denies US claims of mine-clearing ships passage through Strait of Hormuz

title: Hezbollah launches 105 attack waves on Israel — most intense day since war began
description: Alma Research Center reported Hezbollah launched 105 attack waves on Israel on April 11, described as the most intense day of attacks since the war began. Attacks included 30+ rockets targeting Safed, Nahariya, Shlomi, and Karmiel. One Hezbollah drone struck an apartment building in Shlomi (no casualties). Most rockets were intercepted; some hit open areas in the Galilee. Meanwhile, IDF's 98th Division achieved near-complete control of Bint Jbeil with direct clashes against Hezbollah mechanized forces near the town center.

sources.name: JPost — Northern Israel hit with rockets, drones
L'Orient Today — Hezbollah reports direct clashes in Bint Jbeil

title: Israel approves updated target list for energy/infrastructure strikes on Iran
description: Following the collapse of Islamabad talks, Israeli officials confirmed Israel has approved an updated target list of energy and infrastructure sites in Iran in preparation for a scenario where diplomacy fails entirely. Targets include remaining energy infrastructure, potentially power plants and bridges. Iran warned that any attack on its energy facilities would lead to reciprocal attacks that could 'plunge the entire region into complete darkness,' specifically identifying Israel's Orot Rabin thermal power plant (2,590 MW, ~25% of Israel's electricity) as a potential target.

title: Netanyahu corruption trial resumes — court rejects delay request twice
description: Netanyahu's corruption trial resumed at the Jerusalem District Court on April 12 after being paused during the war. Netanyahu filed to delay testimony for two weeks citing 'classified security and diplomatic reasons,' but Justice Rivka Friedman-Feldman rejected the request twice, finding insufficient justification. The court granted only one concession: Monday session can start late at 11:30 AM.

He described killing of Lebanese civilians by the 'Zionist regime' as a war crime. France 'welcomed the ceasefire and emphasized the need to stop attacks against Lebanon.'

description: Israeli airstrikes killed at least 24 people overnight April 11-12 across southern Lebanon. Teffahta (Sidon district): 13 killed. Qana (al-Khashna area): 5 killed, several wounded, residential homes destroyed. Maaroub: 6 killed in airstrike on a house. Dawn strikes also hit Aaitat, Samaya, Mahrouna, Maarakeh, and Khiam (Jallahiya neighborhood) — 3 wounded transferred to Tyre's Hiram Hospital. Strikes intensifying after Islamabad talks collapse.

title: 3 supertankers exit Hormuz — biggest oil movement since war began
description: Three VLCCs (Serifos, Cospearl Lake, He Rong Hai) exited the Strait of Hormuz on April 12, the biggest day of oil exits since the war began. Each VLCC carries ~2M barrels. Cospearl Lake laden with Iraqi oil bound for Zhoushan, China (ETA May 1). Serifos and He Rong Hai loaded Saudi cargo. Ships followed northerly route through Iranian waters along Qeshm/Larak Islands.

Bloomberg — Three oil supertankers sail through Hormuz

title: 2 supertankers U-turn in Hormuz after Islamabad talks collapse
description: Two empty supertankers (Agios Fanourios I and Shalamar) made U-turns in the Strait of Hormuz immediately after the Islamabad peace talks collapsed. Agios Fanourios I headed to Iraq, Shalamar to UAE. The U-turns signal renewed market panic — Monday oil prices expected to spike.

title: Iran: 'no plan for next round of negotiations' after Islamabad collapse

Iran's Foreign Ministry stated 'the ball is in America's court.' The collapse raises risk that the 2-week ceasefire (effective April 8, expiring ~April 22) will not lead to a permanent agreement.

title: Trump announces immediate US naval blockade of Strait of Hormuz
description: President Trump posted on Truth Social announcing 'Effective immediately, the United States Navy, the Finest in the World, will begin the process of BLOCKADING any and all Ships trying to enter, or leave, the Strait of Hormuz.' He also instructed the Navy to 'seek and interdict every vessel in International Waters that has paid a toll to Iran' and to begin 'destroying the mines the Iranians laid in the Straits.' Added: 'At an appropriate moment, we are fully LOCKED AND LOADED, and our Military will finish up the little that is left of Iran.' This represents a major escalation from mine-clearing operations to a full naval counter-blockade, announced hours after the 21-hour Islamabad peace talks collapsed.

The IRGC warned it will 'act against American infrastructure and its partners in a way that will deprive the Americans and its allies of regional oil and gas for years.' Iran's official position described Trump's blockade as 'simply a losing hand' that 'would disastrously backfire.' Fars News confirmed Iran has 'no plan for next round of negotiations.'

Demanded comprehensive end to all hostilities 'from Lebanon to the Red Sea,' calling it a red line. Iran conditioned further talks on full implementation of the Lebanon ceasefire. Over 20 heads of state and 50+ foreign ministers attending the forum.

sources.name: Anadolu Agency — Iran rejects temporary ceasefire
ANI News — Iran Deputy FM demands total end to regional war

title: UN experts condemn Israel's 'unprecedented bombing' in Lebanon after ceasefire announcement
description: UN OHCHR experts condemned Israel's 'unprecedented bombing in Lebanon after ceasefire announcement,' calling for accountability. Statement noted intensified strikes between the ceasefire announcement and its implementation at midnight Beirut time. Separately, Lebanese army officially reported Israeli artillery violations within 30 minutes of ceasefire taking effect.

sources.name: UN OHCHR — Experts condemn Israel's unprecedented bombing
Arab News — Lebanese army accuses Israel of ceasefire violations

title: Brent crude surges past $99/barrel — highest since early March Hormuz closure
description: Brent crude June futures settled at $99.39/barrel (+24.5% from pre-war $79.84), the highest since the initial Hormuz crisis in early March. WTI settled at $93.74 (+24.1%). Prices surged on growing doubts over US-Iran negotiations as ceasefire deadline (April 22) approaches with no Round 2 talks scheduled. EIA raised 2026 Brent forecast to $96/barrel average.

sources.name: Xinhua — Crude futures settle higher April 17
Rigzone — EIA boosts 2026 Brent projection to $96

title: Hezbollah fires 40+ rockets at northern Israel hours before Lebanon ceasefire takes effect
description: Hezbollah launched over 40 rockets at northern Israel in the hours before the 10-day ceasefire took effect at midnight Beirut time. A 25-year-old man was severely wounded by shrapnel in Carmiel after a barrage of 10 rockets. Separate barrages hit Kiryat Shmona. IDF intercepted majority of projectiles.

sources.name: Times of Israel — One hurt as Hezbollah fires over 40 rockets at northern Israel
Al Jazeera — Hezbollah strikes north Israel ahead of ceasefire

title: Iran FM Araghchi declares Strait of Hormuz 'completely open' for commercial vessels during ceasefire
description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi declared the Strait of Hormuz 'completely open' for all commercial vessel traffic during the ceasefire period. Statement: 'In line with the ceasefire in Lebanon, the passage for all commercial vessels through Strait of Hormuz is declared completely open for the remaining period of ceasefire.' Dow futures rallied 500+ points. Oil prices dropped sharply — Brent to $93.59, WTI to $89.90.

sources.name: Bloomberg — Iran Says Hormuz Strait Completely Open for Commercial Ships

title: Hegseth warns Iran: US 'locked and loaded' to strike power plants and energy infrastructure
description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth at Pentagon briefing warned Iran the US is 'locked and loaded' to strike power plants, energy industry, and critical dual-use infrastructure. 'If Iran chooses poorly, then they will have a blockade and bombs dropping on infrastructure, power, and energy.' Gen. Dan Caine: joint force 'postured and ready to resume major combat operations at literally a moment's notice.' 13 ships confirmed turned back by blockade.

title: Iran-UAE back-channel call: VP Mansour bin Zayed speaks with Iran Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf on tensions
description: UAE Vice President Sheikh Mansour bin Zayed Al Nahyan spoke with Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf about reducing regional tensions and discussing regional developments. Notable diplomatic back-channel as Gulf states navigate between US blockade and Iranian pressure.

sources.name: NBC News — Live Updates: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire

title: Pezeshkian declares victory: 'People's steadfastness caused aggressors to retreat during 40-day war'
description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian declared that Iran's people forced the aggressors to retreat during the '40-day war.' Speaking at Ministry of Culture: 'Powers with extensive claims and prestige stood against the Iranian nation, but the people's steadfastness caused these currents to inevitably retreat.' Called for producing narratives about 'the realities and achievements created during the 40-day resistance.'

sources.name: Press TV — Pezeshkian: People's steadfastness forced aggressors to retreat
ANI News — Pezeshkian on conflict

title: Oil crashes on Hormuz opening + ceasefire optimism — Brent falls to $93.59, WTI to $89.90
description: Oil prices reversed sharply after Iran declared Hormuz 'completely open' for commercial traffic and Lebanon ceasefire took hold. Brent crude dropped to $93.59/bbl (+17.2% from pre-war, down from $99.39 earlier). WTI fell to $89.90/bbl (+19.1% from pre-war). Trump said deal with Iran 'very close.' S&P 500 hit record 7,022.95. Dow futures rallied 500+ points after Hormuz announcement. Markets pricing in potential resolution before April 22 ceasefire deadline.

sources.name: Bloomberg — Latest Oil Market News April 17
Invezz — Oil drops on Mideast ceasefire optimism
PBS — Wall Street hits record, S&P 500 rally

title: Israeli drone strike kills 1 in south Lebanon during ceasefire — first fatality since truce began
description: An Israeli drone struck a motorcycle between the southern Lebanese towns of Kounine and Beit Yahoun, killing 1 person and wounding 3 (including a Syrian citizen). First fatality since the 10-day ceasefire took effect at midnight. Lebanese army confirmed the ceasefire violation. UNIFIL observed continued IDF artillery shelling until 6 AM and airspace violations.

sources.name: CBC News — 1 killed in Israeli strike in southern Lebanon
Al Jazeera — What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire

title: Trump declares Israel 'PROHIBITED' from bombing Lebanon — Netanyahu caught off guard
description: Trump posted on Truth Social: 'Israel will not be bombing Lebanon any longer. They are PROHIBITED from doing so by the U.S.A. Enough is enough!!!' Netanyahu and his team were caught off guard — learned about it from media. Axios reported Israeli officials asked White House for clarification as post 'contradicted the agreement.' US official later clarified ceasefire 'preserves Israel's right to self-defense.' Netanyahu responded: 'We haven't finished the job yet.'

sources.name: Reuters — Trump says Israel barred from bombing Lebanon
Axios — Trump shocked Netanyahu with Lebanon post

title: European Hormuz maritime mission — ~30 countries plan UK-led defensive naval force
description: France's Macron and UK's Starmer co-chaired a summit of ~30-40 countries in Paris to plan a multinational defensive mission to secure the Strait of Hormuz. Over a dozen countries offered to contribute naval assets. UK will lead the mission, focused on protecting commercial shipping and mine clearance. Trump responded calling NATO allies 'useless' and telling them to 'STAY AWAY.'

sources.name: Reuters — Over a dozen countries offer role in Hormuz mission
Politico EU — Europe to accelerate Hormuz effort despite Trump

title: Oil crashes to settlement — Brent $90.38, WTI $83.85 — biggest drop since ceasefire Day 1
description: Oil prices settled sharply lower after Iran declared Hormuz open and ceasefire optimism grew. Brent settled at $90.38/bbl (+13.3% from pre-war), down from intraday high of $99.39. WTI settled at $83.85/bbl (+11.1% from pre-war). Biggest single-day drop since April 8 ceasefire. S&P 500 closed at record 7,126.06 (+1.20%). Nasdaq: 13th straight gain (longest since 1992). Dow +868 points. Gas $4.093 (AAA). Sanctions waiver expires April 19.

sources.name: Reuters — Oil falls on Iran Hormuz opening
CNBC — Oil prices, markets April 17

title: US considers $20B cash-for-uranium deal with Iran — Axios exclusive
description: Axios reports the US is considering releasing $20 billion in frozen Iranian funds in exchange for Iran giving up its enriched uranium stockpile (~2,000kg total, including 450kg at 60% purity). Iran initially demanded $27B; US initially offered $6B for humanitarian supplies. Three-page MoU framework being finalized for 60-day negotiation period. Next talks expected this weekend in Islamabad.

sources.name: Axios — US considers $20 billion cash-for-uranium deal
Reuters — Iran-US talks turn to interim deal

title: US strikes Ras Issa oil port in Yemen — 74 killed, 171 injured in deadliest single US strike
description: US forces struck Ras Issa (Ras Isa) port in Hodeidah, Yemen, a Houthi-controlled fuel import hub. Massive fireballs destroyed tanker trucks and port infrastructure. Deadliest single US strike in Operation Rough Rider. Houthi health authorities report 74 killed and 171+ injured. First US attack on a Houthi-controlled oil facility during this campaign. UN Secretary-General Guterres said he was 'gravely concerned.'

sources.name: PBS News — US strikes Yemen oil port
New Arab — Yemen: US strikes kill 74

title: US House war powers vote fails by 1 vote — 213-214
description: US House of Representatives narrowly rejected a resolution directing Trump to end hostilities in Iran by a vote of 213-214. Only Rep. Thomas Massie (R-KY) voted with Democrats; Rep. Jared Golden (D-ME) voted against. War Powers 60-day clock approaches April 29. Senate had also blocked a similar resolution 47-52 on April 15.

sources.name: NBC News — House resolution fails by one vote
Democracy Now — House votes 213-214

title: Oil rebounds sharply April 18 — Brent $95.86, WTI $90.79 — after April 17 crash
description: Oil prices rebounded sharply on April 18 after the massive crash on April 17. Brent traded at $95.86/bbl (+20.1% from pre-war), up from $90.38 settlement. WTI at $90.79/bbl (+20.2% from pre-war), up from $83.85. Rebound driven by realization that US CENTCOM blockade of Iranian ports continues despite Iran declaring Hormuz open. Gas ticks down to $4.076 (AAA). Gold at $4,867/oz. Sanctions waiver expires April 19.

sources.name: Trading Economics — Brent Crude Oil
Investing.com — Brent Oil

title: Pezeshkian publishes open letter to American people — 'Iran harbors no enmity'
description: Iranian President Pezeshkian released a second open letter to the American people, stating Iran harbors no enmity toward ordinary Americans and calling the portrayal of Iran as a threat 'inconsistent with history and current facts.' Warned attacks on Iran's infrastructure would have consequences beyond its borders. Called Iran's military response 'a measured response grounded in legitimate self-defense.'

Tribune India — Pezeshkian on conflict

title: Iran reimposed 'strict control' over Strait of Hormuz — Ghalibaf reverses opening
description: Iran Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf officially reversed the April 17 Hormuz opening, citing US 'untrustworthiness' and 'dark track record.' Iran Armed Forces spokesperson accused US of 'piracy and maritime robbery' under the blockade. Control reverted to IRGC Navy management. Commercial vessels now require prior Iranian coordination to transit.

sources.name: NBC News — Iran reimposed strict control over Hormuz
CBS News — Iran reimposed restrictions on Strait of Hormuz

title: IRGC gunboats fire on tanker in Strait of Hormuz — first shots since ceasefire
description: Two IRGC gunboats opened fire on a tanker attempting to cross the Strait of Hormuz approximately 20 miles NE of Oman. Two additional merchant vessels reported being hit by gunfire. UK Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) confirmed the incident. No injuries or significant damage reported.
location: Strait of Hormuz, 20 miles NE of Oman

title: Trump cites 'good news' on Iran at Arizona rally — blockade remains 'in full force'
description: Trump told Turning Point USA rally in Phoenix that Hormuz is 'fully open and ready for business' (already reversed by Iran). On Air Force One afterward said 'We had some pretty good news 20 minutes ago' but declined to elaborate. Warned ceasefire 'may not be extended without a deal by Wednesday.' Confirmed blockade 'remains in full force until our transaction with Iran is 100% complete.'
location: Phoenix, AZ, USA / Air Force One

title: Iran rejects Trump's claim on enriched uranium handover
description: Iran's Foreign Ministry rejected Trump's claims that Iran agreed to hand over its enriched uranium stockpile, stating it 'will not be transferred anywhere.' Key sticking points remain: US demands 20-year enrichment moratorium vs Iran's offer of 5 years, and disposition of ~450kg of 60% enriched uranium. Next talks expected Monday in Islamabad.

description: US Treasury designated seven commanders of Iran-aligned militia groups (Kata'ib Hizballah, Kata'ib Sayyid Al-Shuhada, Harakat Al-Nujaba, Asa'ib Ahl Al-Haqq) under EO 13224 for planning attacks against US personnel in Iraq. State Department simultaneously designated the commanders.

description: A UNIFIL patrol clearing explosive ordnance near Ghandouriyeh was ambushed with small-arms fire. French Sergeant Major Florian Montorio of the 17th Parachute Engineer Regiment was killed and three soldiers wounded (two seriously). France blamed Hezbollah; Hezbollah denied involvement. Macron demanded Lebanese authorities 'immediately arrest the perpetrators.'

title: Mojtaba Khamenei issues first Army Day message — 'strike like lightning'
description: Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei issued his first Army Day (Farvardin 29) message, declaring Iran was 'courageously defending the land, water, and flag.' description: A container ship was struck by an unidentified projectile approximately 25 nautical miles northeast of Oman. Damage to some containers reported but no fires, no environmental impact, and no injuries. UKMTO confirmed the incident. Ship continued its course. Responsible party unknown. Incident came hours after Iran re-closed Strait of Hormuz.

title: IDF reservist Barak Kalfon dies of wounds from Lebanon explosive
description: Command Sgt.-Maj. (Res.) Barak Kalfon, 48, from Adi, 226th Brigade, died Saturday evening from wounds sustained Friday in an explosion from a pre-planted device in southern Lebanon. IDF investigation determined the device was planted before the ceasefire — not classified as a ceasefire violation. Survived by wife Shimrit and daughters Noga and Maya.

title: IDF soldier Lidor Porat killed in combat in southern Lebanon
description: Sgt. First Class (Res.) Lidor Porat, 31, from Ashdod, 7106th Battalion, 769th Regional Brigade, was killed during combat in southern Lebanon. Multiple additional soldiers were injured (1 severely, 4 moderately, 4 lightly). Second Israeli soldier killed since the Lebanon ceasefire began. IDF cleared for publication April 20.

description: IDF carried out first strikes on Hezbollah since ceasefire took effect April 16-17. Struck a Hezbollah tunnel shaft after identifying operatives entering it south of the Yellow Line. Found cache of explosive devices, rockets, RPGs, and grenades inside. Also struck Hezbollah fighters approaching IDF positions. Hezbollah MP contacted Iran about violations. Trump had said Israel 'prohibited' from bombing, but US clarified defensive strikes allowed.

sources.name: Times of Israel — IDF confirms strikes on Hezbollah operatives

Slammed Washington's 'maximalist' demands and called Iran handing over 440kg enriched uranium stockpile 'a nonstarter.' Spoke at Antalya Diplomacy Forum, Turkey.

description: Kiryat Shmona municipality held a strike to protest the Lebanon ceasefire. Education system shut down. A man left a bag with incendiary material that failed to fully ignite. No injuries. Part of broader pattern of Iran-linked attacks on Jewish targets in London including synagogue in Finchley and ambulances in Golders Green.

description: Israeli forces carried out demolitions in Bint Jbeil, southern Lebanon on Saturday. Continued destruction of infrastructure despite ceasefire in effect since April 16-17.

title: Pezeshkian: 'Trump has no right to deny Iran nuclear rights'
description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian stated Trump 'has no right to deny Iran nuclear rights.' Said Iran 'has no intention of attacking any country' and is exercising 'legitimate and legal right to self-defense.' Added Iran 'does not want to escalate the war and seeks to end it with dignity.' Also apologized to neighboring countries for strikes and promised neighbors will no longer be targeted unless attacks originate from them.

NO MORE MR. NICE GUY!' Posted amid continued Iran refusal to set date for Round 2 talks and Strait of Hormuz re-closure. Ceasefire expires April 22 (3 days).

TIME — Trump Accuses Iran of Total Violation

title: Netanyahu-Milei launch 'Isaac Accords' with Latin America in Jerusalem meeting
description: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu met Argentine President Javier Milei in Jerusalem and announced 'Isaac Accords' — a Latin America-focused diplomatic initiative modeled on the 2020 Abraham Accords. Netanyahu pitched the framework as expanding Israel's Latin American ties amid the Iran war. Argentina is Israel's closest regional partner after Milei's November 2023 election pivot.

sources.name: Times of Israel — Liveblog April 19, 2026

title: Vance-led US delegation to travel to Islamabad Monday for Iran talks
description: Trump confirmed that VP JD Vance, alongside envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, will travel Monday April 20 to Islamabad for the second round of US-Iran ceasefire talks. Pakistan Army Chief Asim Munir is mediating and was in Tehran to secure Iran's participation. US demands include full end to uranium enrichment, transfer of HEU stockpile, end of proxy funding, and relinquishing Hormuz control. Iran's Tasnim reported 'no date' set and conditions on blockade.

sources.name: Washington Times — US reps to travel to Pakistan
Axios — Iran talks Pakistan Witkoff Kushner

title: USS Spruance fires on and seizes Iranian cargo ship Touska in Gulf of Oman
description: US Navy destroyer USS Spruance (DDG-111) intercepted the Iranian-flagged cargo vessel Touska (~900 feet) in the Gulf of Oman after a 6-hour standoff. After the crew failed to comply with warnings, the Spruance fired rounds into the engine room, disabling propulsion. Marines from the 31st MEU then boarded and seized the vessel. The Touska was en route from Port Klang, Malaysia to Chabahar, Iran and was under US Treasury sanctions for IRGC links. First ship physically seized under the US naval blockade. Trump announced the seizure on Truth Social.
location: Gulf of Oman, near Strait of Hormuz

sources.name: NPR — US seizes Iranian cargo ship
CNBC — Trump Navy Iran ship Gulf of Oman
Al Jazeera — Trump says US seized Iran-flagged ship

title: Iran Armed Forces vow retaliation for Touska seizure — 'armed piracy'
description: Iran's Khatam al-Anbiya joint military command officially confirmed the US seizure of the cargo ship Touska, calling it 'armed piracy by the US military' and a violation of the ceasefire agreement. The statement warned: 'The armed forces of the Islamic Republic of Iran will soon respond and retaliate.' The threat comes 2 days before the April 22 ceasefire expiration with no deal in sight.

sources.name: ANI News — Iran confirms Touska seizure, vows retaliation
Al Jazeera — Iran war live

title: IRNA officially declines Pakistan Round 2 talks — cites US 'excessive demands'
description: Iran's official IRNA news agency announced Iran would not attend a new round of negotiations in Pakistan, citing 'Washington's excessive demands, unrealistic expectations, constant shifts in stance, repeated contradictions, and the ongoing naval blockade.' However, contradictory signals emerged with Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf touting 'progress' and unnamed sources telling CNN a delegation would still arrive Tuesday April 22.

NBC News — Iran distance peace deal Hormuz

description: Israeli PM Netanyahu declared that all of Iran's enriched uranium must be removed from the country, either through a negotiated deal or through a resumption of military action. He described the joint US-Israeli campaign as 'the battle of civilization against barbarism' and said Israel is 'ready for Iran war resumption.' Separately, Netanyahu and Argentine President Milei announced the 'Isaac Accords' strategic framework.

description: US military struck the Ras Issa fuel port in western Yemen on April 17, killing at least 74 (some sources report 80-84) and injuring 150-171. The strike targeted Houthi-controlled fuel infrastructure and was the deadliest known US attack on Houthis since Operation Rough Rider began March 15. Human Rights Watch and PBS confirmed casualty figures.

description: Sergeant Major Florian Montorio of France's 17th Parachute Engineer Regiment was killed and 3 other peacekeepers wounded when their UNIFIL ordnance-clearing patrol came under small-arms fire in the village of Ghandouriyeh, southern Lebanon. UN attributed the attack to 'non-state actors' (presumed Hezbollah). Hezbollah denied responsibility. Second European military death in the conflict.

title: IDF establishes 'Yellow Line' in Lebanon — 55 villages blocked, 1,400+ buildings demolished during ceasefire
description: IDF is systematically demolishing civilian homes, public buildings, and schools in southern Lebanon despite the 10-day ceasefire (started April 16). The IDF established a 'Yellow Line' — a 10km military zone stretching north of the border inside Lebanon, modeled on Gaza operations. Paid contractors with heavy equipment are demolishing structures. Residents blocked from returning to at least 55 towns/villages. BBC confirmed 1,400+ buildings destroyed via satellite imagery.
location: Southern Lebanon, south of Litani River

description: No tankers passed through the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday April 20, according to ship tracking data. One of the quietest days in the channel since the conflict began. The combination of the US CENTCOM blockade (Day 8) and Iran's re-closure has brought traffic to a near-complete halt. Only ~6 non-tanker vessels transited. Day 49 of overall Hormuz disruption.

They allegedly passed F-15 engine diagrams and pilot photographs to Iranian handlers. Eight additional soldiers are suspected of knowing about the espionage without reporting it. The case is part of what Israeli intelligence calls a 400% increase in Iranian recruitment attempts since October 2023.

sources.name: Jerusalem Post — IAF technicians charged with spying for Iran

title: Iran Air resumes domestic flights after 50-day suspension
description: Iran Air resumed domestic flights after a 50-day suspension, starting with the Tehran-Mashhad corridor. The resumption of domestic air services is a sign of partial normalization in Iran despite the ongoing conflict.

sources.name: Xinhua — Iran Air resumes domestic flights

title: Pentagon removes 15 wounded troops from Iran war casualty list without explanation
description: The Pentagon's Defense Casualty Analysis System dropped 15 wounded-in-action troops from its casualty rolls on Tuesday without public comment, reducing the total from 428 to 413. Two Pentagon spokespeople were unable to explain the removal. The Intercept reported this as part of a broader pattern of casualty underreporting.

sources.name: The Intercept — Pentagon erases wounded troops from Iran war casualty list

title: Lebanon to request one-month ceasefire extension at Thursday Washington talks
description: Lebanon will request a one-month extension of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire at Thursday talks at the State Department. Israeli Ambassador Yechiel Leiter and Lebanese Ambassador Nada Hamadeh Mouawad will meet with Secretary of State Marco Rubio. Lebanon demands: end of Israeli bombing in occupied areas, commitment to ceasefire terms. Current 10-day ceasefire set to expire Sunday April 26.

sources.name: Jerusalem Post — Lebanon to propose extending ceasefire during Washington talks
Times of Israel — Beirut to request one-month ceasefire extension

title: Trump says 'no time frame' on Iran war, denies midterm politics driving decisions
description: President Trump stated there is 'no time frame' for the conflict with Iran and rejected suggestions that the 2026 midterm elections are influencing his approach. Separately, Trump posted on Truth Social that 'Iran is collapsing financially' and 'losing 500 Million Dollars a day.' White House Press Secretary Leavitt stated Iran must hand over its enriched uranium as part of negotiations.

sources.name: CNN — Trump no time frame Iran war
PBS — Timeline of Trump's shifting statements on Iran war

title: Iran releases video of IRGC seizing MSC Francesca and Epaminondas in Strait of Hormuz
description: Iran released footage of IRGC Navy seizing two container ships — the MSC Francesca (Panama-flagged, Italian-owned, 11,312 TEU) and the Epaminondas (Liberian-flagged) — accusing them of tampering with navigation systems and transiting without authorization. A third vessel (Euphoria) was fired upon but not seized. Both ships now stationary off Iranian coast. Panama condemned the seizure as 'illegal' and 'a grave attack against maritime security.' RPG damage to Epaminondas bridge.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Iran releases video of IRGC seizing ship
CNN — Video shows Iranian soldiers seizing ships
Times of Israel — Iran seizes pair of container ships

title: Satellite imagery reveals 33 IRGC fast-attack boats near Kargan coast
description: Sentinel-2 satellite imagery captured by OSINT investigator Mehdi H. shows at least 33 IRGC 'mosquito fleet' fast-attack craft returning to bases north of the Strait of Hormuz after conducting patrols. Vessels likely armed with short-range anti-ship missiles and artillery rockets. Analysts warn IRGC boats could be exploring possibility of destroying undersea cables.
location: Near Kargan coast, north of Strait of Hormuz

sources.name: The Week — Satellite image captures 33 IRGC fast-attack fleet near Kargan coast
The War Zone — Iran ramps up attacks on ships

title: Iran banks first Strait of Hormuz toll revenue
description: Deputy parliament speaker Hamidreza Hajibabaei announced the first revenue from Iran's Strait of Hormuz shipping toll system has been deposited into the Central Bank account. No details on amount collected or who paid. Iran charges tolls based on cargo type and risk level.

sources.name: Free Malaysia Today — Iran receives first revenue from Strait of Hormuz tolls
Free Press Journal — Iran receives first Strait of Hormuz toll revenue

title: IRGC-linked media maps 7 undersea internet cables in Hormuz — implicit threat to Gulf infrastructure
description: IRGC-linked Tasnim news agency published detailed mapping of 7 undersea internet cables running through the Strait of Hormuz. Gulf states (UAE, Qatar, Bahrain, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia) rely on these cables for 90%+ of internet, banking, and cloud services. Iran relies on them for less than 40%. Analysts describe it as a thinly veiled threat. Cutting cables could affect Gulf states, India, and Europe.

sources.name: Iran International — IRGC-linked media hints at threat to undersea cables
Stimson Center — Beneath the Strait: Iran Could Threaten Gulf Data Centers

title: Israel demolishes homes across occupied southern Lebanon buffer zone
description: Israel conducting large-scale home demolitions across 10km buffer zone in occupied southern Lebanon, claiming homes used as Hezbollah outposts. Village of Beit Lif 'almost entirely flattened' per AP journalists on scene. UNIFIL warns displaced populations will have nowhere to return. Israel established 'Yellow Line' military zone ~10km north of border. Demolitions form major agenda item for Washington talks. Lebanon calls demolitions ceasefire violations.
location: Beit Lif, Khiam, Bint Jbeil — southern Lebanon

Common Dreams — Israel continues 'Gaza tactics' in Lebanon

title: US seizes M/T Majestic X tanker carrying sanctioned Iranian oil in Indian Ocean
description: Pentagon announced US military seized another tanker (M/T Majestic X) carrying sanctioned Iranian oil in the Indian Ocean. Statement: 'We will continue global maritime enforcement to disrupt illicit networks.' Second seizure outside Middle East theater, expanding enforcement beyond Hormuz.

title: Pezeshkian accuses US of 'hypocritical rhetoric,' blames blockade for stalled talks
description: Iranian President Pezeshkian posted on X that Tehran 'seeks dialogue and agreement' but accused the US of 'breach of commitments, blockade and threats' as main obstacles. Stated: 'the world sees your endless hypocritical rhetoric and contradiction between claims and actions.' Parliament speaker Ghalibaf reiterated Hormuz will not reopen while US naval blockade remains.

title: Iran Air resumes domestic flights after 50-day suspension — Tehran to Mashhad
description: Iran Air resumed domestic flights on the Tehran-Mashhad corridor after a 50-day suspension since Feb 28 airspace closure. First flight departed Mehrabad at 10 AM local, return at 12:30 PM. Tehran Imam Khomeini and Mehrabad airports reopened April 20. Additional airports (Urmia, Kermanshah, Abadan, Shiraz, Kerman, Rasht, Yazd, Zahedan, Gorgan, Birjand) expected to resume from April 25. Mashhad Airport also resumed international flights via Varesh Airlines.

title: Lebanon-Israel Round 2 talks begin at State Department — Lebanon demands full withdrawal
description: Lebanese Ambassador Nada Hamadeh Moawad and Israeli Ambassador Yechiel Leiter hold second session of direct talks — only second such direct meeting in three decades. Led by Secretary of State Rubio and envoy Huckabee. Lebanon demands: full ceasefire, Israeli troop withdrawal from southern Lebanon, prisoner releases, Lebanese army border deployment, reconstruction. Lebanon PM: 'no talks without Israeli withdrawal.'

PBS News — What to know about new direct talks

title: S&P 500 closes above 7,000 for first time — markets erase all Iran war losses
description: S&P 500 closed at 7,137.90 (+1.05%) above 7,000 for the first time ever. Nasdaq hit record at 24,657.57 (+1.64%) with a 13-session winning streak — longest since 1992. Markets have fully erased all Iran war losses. Rally driven by Trump extending ceasefire indefinitely and strong earnings season (75% beat rate). Analysts warn optimism may be misplaced while Hormuz remains closed.

sources.name: CNN — Stocks at record highs shrugging off war
Yahoo Finance — S&P 500 closes above 7,000 first time

title: CENTCOM reports 31 vessels turned back in Iran naval blockade — up from 29
description: CENTCOM announced it has directed 31 vessels, mostly oil tankers, to turn around or return to port as part of the US naval blockade of Iranian ports. Up from 29 reported earlier. Blockade operation involves 10,000+ troops, 17 warships, and 100+ aircraft. Day 10 of CENTCOM blockade (started April 13).

title: Trump orders US Navy to 'shoot and kill' any Iranian boat laying mines in Strait of Hormuz
description: President Trump said on Truth Social he has ordered the US Navy to 'shoot and kill' any Iranian boat laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz. 'We have total control over the Strait of Hormuz. No ship can enter or leave without the approval of the United States Navy. It is Sealed up Tight, until such time as Iran is able to make a DEAL!!!' Pentagon previously told Congress clearing the strait of mines could take up to 6 months if extensively laid.

Joins USS Gerald R. Ford (Red Sea) and USS Abraham Lincoln (Arabian Sea). Bush transited via Cape of Good Hope — a 6,000-mile detour around Africa to avoid Houthi threats in Bab el-Mandeb. Times of Israel reports third carrier arrives as Trump says 'clock is ticking' for Iran.

sources.name: Times of Israel — Third aircraft carrier arrives in Mideast

Phelan had reportedly clashed with Hegseth over accelerating warship production. Announcement came as Hegseth said the blockade will continue 'as long as it takes' and US remains 'locked and loaded' for further attacks on Iran energy facilities.

Called for 'a new attitude and culture for peace' and condemned capital punishment during the press conference. Rejected any regime change solution.

Original 10-day truce was set to expire Sunday April 26; now extended through mid-May. Trump said Netanyahu and Lebanese President Aoun will come to Washington 'over the next couple of weeks.' VP Vance, Rubio, and Ambassadors Huckabee and Issa participated.

description: Hezbollah fired approximately 4 rockets at the northern Israeli border community of Shtula, the first rocket attack on Israeli territory during the ceasefire. All rockets were intercepted by air defense systems. No casualties. Hezbollah said it was in response to Israeli artillery shelling of Yater in southern Lebanon. IDF destroyed the launcher used and called it a 'blatant violation of the ceasefire agreement.'
