sources.name: PBS — Trump says he won't use nuclear weapons in Iran
US News — Iran to make offer aimed at satisfying US demands, Trump tells Reuters

title: Araghchi arrives in Islamabad; Iran FM spokesperson denies US talks planned
description: Iran FM Abbas Araghchi arrived in Islamabad Friday evening with a small delegation for regional tour (Pakistan, Oman, Moscow). Received by Pakistan Deputy PM Ishaq Dar and Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir. However, Iran FM spokesperson Baghaei said: 'No meeting is planned to take place between Iran and the US.' Iran framed visit as bilateral consultations. Witkoff and Kushner confirmed departing Saturday for Islamabad for direct talks.

sources.name: Dawn — Iranian delegation led by FM Araghchi lands in Islamabad
PressTV — Iran FM to embark on regional tour to Pakistan, Oman, Russia

title: IRGC issues 'eye for head' threat — 'fingers on trigger, target bank ready'
description: IRGC Aerospace and Navy commanders issued a joint message: 'The bank of targets is specified, fingers are on the trigger, and our backing is the unity of the streets and the cohesion of officials.' Vowed 'eye for head' reprisal if US resumes aggression after ceasefire.

sources.name: PressTV — Tehran warns US of 'eye for head' reprisal
Kurdistan24 — IRGC signals end of restraint, threatens US interests

title: Netanyahu accuses Hezbollah of trying to 'sabotage historic peace' with Lebanon
description: Netanyahu released video statement accusing Hezbollah of trying to sabotage historic peace between Israel and Lebanon. Said: 'We maintain full freedom of action against any threat, including emerging ones. We attacked yesterday and we attacked today.' Stated the process to achieve 'historic peace between Israel and Lebanon' has begun. Said he had an 'excellent conversation' with Trump.

sources.name: Times of Israel — Netanyahu accuses Hezbollah of trying to sabotage truce
Haaretz — Netanyahu: Hezbollah trying to derail historic peace

title: Hezbollah MP Fayyad: ceasefire is 'meaningless' — Hezbollah not party to negotiations
description: Hezbollah MP Ali Fayyad 'firmly rejects' the Lebanon ceasefire, calling it 'meaningless in light of Israel's continued escalation of hostile actions — assassinations, bombardment, and opening fire — as well as its ongoing destructive annihilation of Lebanese border villages.' Hezbollah was not party to the ceasefire negotiations in Washington.

sources.name: Washington Examiner — Hezbollah lawmaker firmly rejects Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
Al-Monitor — Hezbollah MP ceasefire meaningless in light of Israeli attacks

title: China rejects Trump's 'gift from China' claim about seized Iranian ship Touska
description: Chinese FM Guo Jiakun rejected Trump's claim that the seized M/V Touska ship was carrying a 'gift from China': 'China opposes any accusations and associations that lack a factual basis.' Stated 'Normal international trade relations between countries should not be subject to interference.' Iran's Red Crescent separately claimed the Touska carried vital kidney dialysis supplies.

sources.name: Al-Monitor — China rejects Trump accusation intercepted Iran ship was gift from China
US News — China rejects Trump accusation

title: Wizz Air cancels planned April 25 resumption of Israel flights — extends suspension to May 4
description: Wizz Air cancelled its planned April 25 resumption of Tel Aviv flights, extending the suspension through May 4 citing security concerns and possibility of ceasefire collapse. The airline had been one of few carriers planning to restart Israel service.
location: Ben Gurion Airport, Tel Aviv, Israel

sources.name: Globes — Wizz Air cancels restoration of Israel flights
Ynet — Wizz Air walks back plan to resume Israel flights

title: 6 killed in Israeli strikes across southern Lebanon on Day 57 — ceasefire violations continue
description: Israeli strikes across southern Lebanon killed at least 6 people and injured 2 more on April 25 (Day 57), per Lebanese Health Ministry. This is in addition to the 2 killed in Touline the previous day. Over 30 Hezbollah operatives killed and hundreds of Hezbollah sites destroyed since ceasefire took effect April 17, per IDF cumulative statement.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Israel continues attacks on Lebanon despite extension of ceasefire

title: US Treasury freezes $344M in IRGC/Hezbollah cryptocurrency on Tron blockchain
description: OFAC sanctioned two Tron blockchain addresses, freezing $344 million in USDT linked to IRGC and Hezbollah financing networks. Tether cooperated by freezing the assets. Treasury Secretary Bessent: 'We will follow the money.' Largest crypto enforcement action linked to the Iran war.

sources.name: CNN — US freezes $344 million in cryptocurrency linked to Iran
Bloomberg Law — Bessent: US freezes $344M in crypto with sanctions tied to Iran

title: US sanctions Hengli Petrochemical (China) and 40 shipping entities for Iran oil trade
description: Treasury OFAC sanctioned Hengli Petrochemical (Dalian) Refinery Co. — one of China's biggest independent refineries (400,000 bpd capacity) and one of Iran's largest crude oil customers. Also sanctioned 19 shadow fleet vessels and ~40 shipping firms involved in transporting Iranian crude, LPG, and petroleum products under 'Operation Economic Fury.'

sources.name: US Treasury — Economic Fury targets global network fueling Iran's oil trade
Washington Times — US imposes sanctions on China-based oil refinery, 40 shippers

title: Bessent: US will not renew Iranian or Russian oil waivers — Iran may shutter production in days
description: Treasury Secretary Bessent announced the US will not renew waivers for Russian oil shipments in transit, and any extension for Iranian oil is 'completely out of the question.' On Iran: 'We have the blockade, and there's no oil coming out. In the next two, three days, they're going to have to start shuttering production, which will be very bad for their wells.'

sources.name: Washington Times — US won't renew Iranian and Russian oil waivers
ABC News — US won't renew Iranian and Russian oil waivers

title: Iran resumes international flights from Tehran — first in over 50 days
description: Iran resumed international passenger flights from Tehran's Imam Khomeini International Airport after a 50+ day hiatus. First flights to Istanbul, Muscat, and Beijing. Six airports reopened including IKIA, Mehrabad, and Mashhad, plus 10 additional airports for domestic service. Marks a significant de-escalation signal ahead of potential Islamabad Round 2 talks.

sources.name: PressTV — Iran resumes international passenger flights after 50-day hiatus
Middle East Monitor — Iran to resume international flights to Istanbul, Muscat
